design_philosophy.core_loop: Select stage, navigate platforming challenges, defeat Robot Master boss to acquire weapon, use weapon weakness chain to optimize future boss encounters, progress through fortress stages
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Non-linear stage selection with asymmetric boss weaknesses creates strategic decision-making; player must discover optimal order or face difficulty spikes
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a versatile combat android equipped with collected powers; explore themed mining worlds; uncover the mystery of Break Man/Proto Man
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The game enforces weapon weakness hierarchy and optional stage order—players who sequence stages poorly face significantly harder bosses, rewarding pattern recognition and planning over raw execution. Introduces Rush companion abilities locked behind stage completion, gating mobility options.

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with extra lives obtainable through power-ups and point milestones
difficulty.continues_system: Password system allows return to game state, effectively providing unlimited continues with progression preservation
difficulty.checkpointing: Password-based checkpointing; players can save progress between major stage completions
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: non-linear stage selection allows players to sequence encounters strategically; weapon weakness chain permits boss difficulty optimization; Rush abilities gate certain shortcuts based on stage completion order

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon acquisition from defeated Robot Masters; Rush transformation unlocks (Coil, Jet, Marine) based on stage completion; E-Tanks for resource management
progression.unlock_mechanism: Defeating Robot Masters unlocks their weapons; completing stages unlocks Rush transformations; Break Man encounters gate progression through fortress
replayability_hooks: Weapon weakness discovery encourages stage re-sequencing
Multiple Rush transformations enable alternate traversal routes
Doc Robots stage rematch with previous Robot Master abilities
Password system preserves mid-game states for experimentation

world_building_techniques: Stage theming aligned with Robot Master identities (Top Man's spinning world, Shadow Man's darkness, etc.)
Break Man encounters hint at larger mystery gradually revealed in ending
Narrative framing: peace-keeping project Gamma stolen, Wily's reformation proves false
Final revelation of Break Man as Proto Man (Mega Man's brother) recontextualizes earlier encounters

boss_design.philosophy: Each Robot Master embodies thematic identity with stage design and weapon mechanic reflecting their name/purpose; weakness hierarchy creates strategic depth; Break Man encounters serve as mini-boss gates with narrative significance
notable_bosses: Top Man (spinning environment, defeat with Bottom Throw weapon from other masters)
Break Man/Proto Man (recurring mini-boss, mechanical equal to Mega Man)
Doc Robots (rematch stage featuring previous game's Robot Master abilities)

faction_indicators: Dr. Light's lab vs. Dr. Wily's fortress (recurring hero/villain dichotomy)
Proto Man as ambiguous third faction (neither fully ally nor enemy until reveal)
Robot Masters as independent agents initially, enslaved/corrupted by Wily
danger_signals: Wily's false reformation attempt signals unreliable authority
Haywire Robot Masters indicate loss of control/societal breakdown
Gamma peace-keeping robot weaponized for conquest—technology misuse theme
environmental_storytelling: Stage aesthetics reflect Robot Master identity (mining worlds thematically distinct)
Dr. Wily's fortress architecture escalates from Robot Master domains to final stronghold
Rush companion visually represents Dr. Light's technological benevolence
Break Man's mysterious appearances create breadcrumb trail of narrative intrigue
